sn.s: S'Schwätzli ghört zum Alltag in Luzärn.
sn.e: Small talk is part of everyday life in Lucerne.

sn.s: Mer redet über s'Wätter, wie überall uf de Wält.
sn.e: You talk about the weather, like everywhere in the world.

sn.s: "Schöns Wätter hüt, gäll?" isch immer guet.
sn.e: "Nice weather today, isn't it?" always works.
Or: "Hopefully it won't rain at the weekend."

sn.s: In de Migros, im Tram, beim Beck: überall es Schwätzli.
sn.e: In the supermarket, on the tram, at the baker's: small talk everywhere.

sn.s: Es mues nöd tüüf sii, nu fründlich und kurz.
sn.e: It doesn't need to be deep, just friendly and short.

sn.s: Mer fragt: "Und, alls guet bi der?"
sn.e: You ask: "So, all good with you?"
The answer is mostly: "Yes, can't complain."

sn.s: "Mues ja" heisst, es gaht scho, au wenn's mängisch hä.
sn.e: "Mues ja" means it's alright, even if sometimes it isn't.

sn.s: Am Aafang han ich z'viel erzellt uf so e Frog.
sn.e: At the start I told too much in answer to such a question.

sn.s: Aber es Schwätzli isch kurz, das han ich glärnt.
sn.e: But small talk is short, I learned that.

sn.s: Es git de Lüt s'Gfühl, mer ghört zäme.
sn.e: It gives people the feeling that you belong together.

sn.s: Es chliis Wort cha en ganze Tag besser mache.
sn.e: A small word can make a whole day better.

sn.s: Jetz mach ich's sälber, ganz natürli.
sn.e: Now I do it myself, quite naturally.
